name: takeshicc
description: Quickly send file references to Claude Code in your terminal, and browse past sessions.

takeshicc.sessions.tailLines.markdownDescription: Number of lines of the most recent message to show under each session title in the sidebar. `0` hides the tail entirely. The tail is refreshed on the slow auto-refresh tick (~30s), not on every hook event.

takeshicc.layout.sidebarSizePx.markdownDescription: Sidebar width in pixels written to VS Code's global `state.vscdb` (key `workbench.sideBar.size`) when running `Takeshi CC: Apply Layout Sizes`. Requires a window reload.

takeshicc.layout.panelSizePx.markdownDescription: Panel width (or height, when on bottom/top) in pixels written to VS Code's global `state.vscdb` (key `workbench.panel.size`) when running `Takeshi CC: Apply Layout Sizes`. Requires a window reload.

takeshicc.worktrees.bootstrapCommand.markdownDescription: Shell command to run in the new worktree's directory immediately after `git worktree add` succeeds. Leave empty to skip. Available placeholders: `{new_branch}`, `{worktree_path}`, `{base_branch}` (literal `{` is written as `{{`). Runs via the system shell (`cmd.exe` on Windows, `/bin/sh` on Unix) in the background — multiple creates can have bootstraps in flight at once. Each worktree row shows a spinner in place of its delete button until that bootstrap exits; row selection and deletion are blocked for the path during that window. On exit, a VS Code info notification fires for success or an error notification (with a link to the Takeshi CC output channel) for failure. Example: `npm install && cp ../main/.env .env`.

scripts.vsix: npm run package && npm run rebuild:electron && vsce package
